Tacrolimus + MMF + Pred = standard triple therapy. Ciclosporin (older CNI) rarely used now. Belatacept (CTLA4-Ig) alternative CNI-sparing (monthly IV) — better GFR, less CNI toxicity, but higher acute rejection, EBV risk.

CNI-Sparing / Alternative Regimens

RegimenUse Case
Tacrolimus + MMF (steroid-free/withdrawal)Selected low-risk; avoid steroid side effects
Belatacept + MMF + PredCNI toxicity (nephrotoxicity, diabetes, neurotoxicity); EBV seropositive only
Ciclosporin + MMF + PredTacrolimus intolerance (neurotoxicity, diabetes)
Sirolimus/Everolimus (mTOR) + CNI reductionMalignancy risk reduction; wound healing issues (avoid early post-Tx)

7. Rejection

Acute Cellular Rejection (ACR) — Banff Classification

GradeHistologyTreatment
BorderlineMild tubulitis, minimal inflammationOptimise CNI; consider steroid pulse
IAModerate tubulitis (t2), interstitial infiltrate (i2)Methylprednisolone 500mg IV × 3 days
IBSevere tubulitis (t3), interstitial infiltrate (i3)Methylprednisolone 500mg IV × 3–5 days ± ATG if steroid-resistant
IIA/IIBArterial inflammation (v1/v2) — vascular rejectionATG (steroids alone inadequate)
IIITransmural arteritis (v3) — severe vascularATG + plasma exchange ± IVIG

Antibody-Mediated Rejection (AMR)

Criteria (Banff)Treatment
DSA + C4d+ (or ptc >0) + microvascular inflammation (g+ptc)Plasma exchange + IVIG + rituximab ± bortezomib (refractory); eculizumab (severe)
Chronic AMRTransplant glomerulopathy (cg >0) + DSA; difficult to treat; optimise CNI, consider belatacept

Monitoring for Rejection

ParameterFrequency
Serum Creatinine2–3x/week early; weekly × 1mo; monthly × 6mo; then q3mo
Donor-Specific Antibodies (DSA)q3–6mo (or if Cr rise); Luminex single antigen bead
Protocol Biopsy3–6mo, 1yr (some centres) — detects subclinical rejection
UrinalysisProteinuria, haematuria

8. Post-Transplant Complications

Infection (Commonest Cause of Death Year 1)

TimeframeTypical Infections
0–1 monthSurgical (wound, UTI, pneumonia), donor-derived, HSV, CMV (if D+/R–)
1–6 monthsCMV (peak), EBV, BK virus, PJP, fungal (Candida, Aspergillus), community-acquired
>6 monthsCommunity-acquired (UTI, pneumonia, skin), chronic viral (BK, CMV), TB

Prophylaxis (Standard):

InfectionProphylaxis
PJPTMP-SMX 1 year (or dapsone/atovaquone if allergic)
CMVvalganciclovir 3–6mo (D+/R– highest risk); pre-emptive monitoring alternative
HSV/VZVAciclovir/valaciclovir 3mo
FungalFluconazole (if high risk)
BK VirusNo prophylaxis; screen plasma/urine PCR q3mo × 2yr

Malignancy (Leading Cause of Death After Year 1)

CancerRiskScreening
Skin (SCC, BCC, Melanoma)50–100x (sun protection, dermatology q6–12mo)
PTLD (Post-Transplant Lymphoproliferative Disorder)EBV-driven; early (<1yr) = polymorphic; late = monomorphicEBV PCR monitoring; reduce immunosuppression 1st step
Kaposi SarcomaHHV-8; reduce immunosuppression ± sirolimus
Renal Cell CarcinomaIn native kidneys (especially ADPKD, ESRD)US native kidneys q1–2yr
Cervical/AnalHPV; enhanced screening

Cardiovascular Disease

Risk FactorManagement
Hypertension<130/80; CCB + ACEi/ARB (if allograft function allows); avoid CNI-induced
DyslipidaemiaStatin (atorvastatin 20–40mg) — interacts with CNI (dose adjust tacrolimus)
DiabetesNew-onset diabetes after transplant (NODAT) — tacrolimus > ciclosporin; steroids; lifestyle + metformin/SGLT2i/GLP-1 RA
ObesityWeight management

Recurrent Disease (in Allograft)

DiseaseRecurrence RateManagement
FSGS30–50% (early, proteinuria)Plasmapheresis + rituximab + CNI optimisation
IgA Nephropathy20–30% (late)Optimise BP, ACEi/ARB, SGLT2i, fish oil
Membranous Nephropathy<10% (if anti-PLA2R+)Monitor anti-PLA2R; treat if recurrence
C3G / aHUSHigh (complement-mediated)Eculizumab/ravulizumab prophylaxis/pre-emptive
OxalosisVery highCombined liver-kidney transplant
ATTR AmyloidosisNil (donor liver clears)Liver-kidney if variant

9. BK Virus Nephropathy (BKVN)

FeatureDetail
ScreeningPlasma/urine BK PCR q3mo × 2yr (then annually)
DiagnosisPlasma BK >10⁴ copies/mL + allograft dysfunction + biopsy (SV40 T-antigen +)
Decoy CellsUrine cytology — suspicious but not diagnostic
TreatmentReduce immunosuppression (↓ MMF/stop → ↓ Tac); lefuoflovir/quinolones (weak evidence); IVIG; cidofovir (nephrotoxic)

10. Pregnancy Post-Transplant

RecommendationDetail
TimingWait ≥1 year post-transplant (stable graft, low immunosuppression)
ImmunosuppressionStop MMF (teratogenic) → switch to AZA/CNI/Pred pre-conception; Tacrolimus/CsA/Pred/AZA safe; Stop mTOR (sirolimus/everolimus)
MonitoringMonthly Cr, BP, tacrolimus levels, DSA; obstetric + transplant joint care
OutcomesGood if graft function stable; ↑ preeclampsia, preterm, SGA

11. High-Yield FCPS/MRCP Points

[!important]
- Transplant = treatment of choice for ESRD; live donor > deceased
- Recipient workup: CV, infection (HIV/HBV/HCV/CMV/TB), malignancy, HLA/PRA/DSA, psychosocial
- Living donor: GFR >90, proteinuria <300mg, BP <140/90, single artery preferred
- Induction: basiliximab (low risk) vs ATG (high risk/sensitised)
- Maintenance: Tacrolimus + MMF + Pred (standard); target Tac trough 5–10 ng/mL
- Rejection: ACR = steroid pulse ± ATG; AMR = PLEX + IVIG + rituximab + DSA monitoring
- Infection: 0–1mo (surgical), 1–6mo (CMV, BK, PJP), >6mo (community); prophylaxis: TMP-SMX, valganciclovir, aciclovir
- Malignancy: skin cancer (SCC/BCC) 50–100x; PTLD (EBV); Kaposi (HHV-8); screen skin q6–12mo
- CVD: BP <130/80, statin (interacts with tacrolimus), NODAT (tacrolimus risk)
- BK virus: screen PCR q3mo × 2yr; plasma >10⁴ + biopsy = BKVN; ↓ immunosuppression
- Recurrent disease: FSGS (30–50%, PLEX+RTX), IgA (20–30%), aHUS (eculizumab)
- Pregnancy: wait ≥1yr; stop MMF/mTOR; Tac/CsA/Pred/AZA safe

12. Common Confusions / Exam Traps

TrapCorrection
All patients get basiliximabHigh risk (PRA>80%, DSA, retransplant, DCD) → ATG
Tacrolimus trough same alwaysEarly (1–3mo): 8–12; Late: 5–8 ng/mL
MMF dose fixed 1g BD1000–1500mg BD; reduce if leucopenia/GI side effects
Steroids stopped at 3moTaper to 5mg by 3–6mo; many continue low-dose lifelong
CNI = only nephrotoxicityAlso neurotoxicity (tremor, headache, seizures), diabetes, hypertension, hyperkalaemia, hyperuricaemia
Belatacept = for everyoneEBV seropositive only; higher acute rejection; monthly IV
DSA = always rejectionCan be de novo non-complement fixing; monitor trend
Protocol biopsy = routine everywhereNot universal; centre-dependent
BKVN = treat with antivirals1st step = reduce immunosuppression (↓ MMF, ↓ Tac)
Pregnancy = stop all immunosuppressionStop MMF/mTOR only; continue Tac/CsA/Pred/AZA
Graft survival = patient survivalDeath with functioning graft common (CVD, infection, malignancy)

13. Mnemonics

  • Induction: Basiliximab = Low Risk; ATG = High Risk = BL-HR
  • Maintenance: Tacrolimus + MMF + Pred = TMP
  • Tac Trough: Early 8–12, Late 5–8 = EL
  • Rejection: ACR = Steroids ± ATG; AMR = PLEX + IVIG + Rituximab = AS-APIR
  • Infection Timeline: 0–1mo Surgical, 1–6mo CMV/BK/PJP, >6mo Community = S-C-P
  • Prophylaxis: TMP-SMX (PJP), Valganciclovir (CMV), Aciclovir (HSV) = TVA
  • Malignancy: Skin (SCC/BCC), PTLD (EBV), Kaposi (HHV-8) = SPK
  • BK Virus: Screen PCR q3mo × 2yr; Plasma >10⁴ = BKVN; ↓ Immunosuppression = S2P↓
  • Recurrent FSGS: Plasmapheresis + Rituximab = PR
  • Pregnancy: MMF OUT, mTOR OUT; Tac IN = MO-MO-TI

14. Mind Map

mindmap
  root((Kidney Transplantation))
    Indications
      ESRD_G5[On dialysis or imminent]
      Pre_emptive[Before dialysis - best]
      Contraindications[Active cancer, infection, severe CVD]
    Recipient_Workup
      CV[ECG, Echo, Stress, Angio]
      Infection[HIV, HBV, HCV, CMV, TB]
      Malignancy[Age-appropriate screens]
      Immunology[HLA, PRA, DSA, Crossmatch]
      Psychosocial[Adherence, support]
    Donor
      Living[GFR>90, Prot<300, BP<140/90, Single artery]
      Deceased[DBD, DCD, ECD (KDPI>85%)]
      Paired_Exchange[Incompatible pairs swap]
    Allocation
      HLA_Match[Lower mismatch = better]
      PRA/DSA[Sensitised = hard to match]
      KDPI[Lower = better; ECD >85%]
    Immunosuppression
      Induction[Basiliximab low-risk; ATG high-risk]
      Maintenance[Tac + MMF + Pred (standard)]
      Targets[Tac 5-10, MMF 1-1.5g BD, Pred 5mg]
      Alternatives[Belatacept (EBV+), mTOR, steroid-free]
    Rejection
      ACR[Banff IA/IB/II/III; Steroids ± ATG]
      AMR[DSA + C4d + g/ptc; PLEX + IVIG + RTX]
      Monitoring[Cr, DSA, Protocol biopsy]
    Complications
      Infection[0-1mo Surgical; 1-6mo CMV/BK; >6mo Community]
      Prophylaxis[TMP-SMX, Valganciclovir, Aciclovir]
      Malignancy[Skin 50-100x; PTLD EBV; Kaposi HHV-8]
      CVD[BP<130/80, Statin (CNI interaction), NODAT]
      BKVirus[PCR q3mo x2yr; >10^4 = BKVN; ↓ IS]
      Recurrence[FSGS 30-50% PLEX+RTX; IgA 20-30%]
      Pregnancy[Wait 1yr; Stop MMF/mTOR; Tac/CsA/Pred/AZA]

15. 24-Hour Recall Prompts

  1. Transplant = treatment of choice for ESRD; live donor > deceased
  2. Recipient workup: CV, infection screen, malignancy, HLA/PRA/DSA
  3. Living donor: GFR >90, proteinuria <300mg, BP <140/90, single artery
  4. Induction: basiliximab (low risk) vs ATG (high risk/sensitised)
  5. Maintenance: Tac + MMF + Pred; Tac trough early 8–12, late 5–8
  6. ACR: Banff grades; steroid pulse ± ATG
  7. AMR: DSA + C4d + inflammation; PLEX + IVIG + rituximab
  8. Infection timeline: 0–1mo surgical, 1–6mo CMV/BK/PJP, >6mo community
  9. Prophylaxis: TMP-SMX (PJP), valganciclovir (CMV), aciclovir (HSV)
  10. Malignancy: skin 50–100x, PTLD (EBV), Kaposi (HHV-8); skin screen q6–12mo
  11. CVD: BP <130/80, statin (CNI interaction), NODAT
  12. BK virus: PCR q3mo × 2yr; plasma >10⁴ = BKVN; reduce IS
  13. Recurrent FSGS: 30–50%; PLEX + rituximab
  14. Pregnancy: wait ≥1yr; STOP MMF/mTOR; Tac/CsA/Pred/AZA safe

16. 7-Day / 15-Day / 30-Day Revision Tracker

DayDateRecall (1-5)Notes
1
7
15
30

17. Must Know / Should Know / Nice to Know

PriorityContent
Must Know 🔴Indications/contraindications, recipient/donor workup, induction/maintenance regimens, ACR/AMR diagnosis & treatment, infection timeline & prophylaxis, malignancy risks, BK virus, recurrent FSGS, pregnancy
Should Know 🟡Belatacept, mTOR inhibitors, steroid withdrawal, protocol biopsy, desensitisation protocols, DCD/ECD outcomes, PTLD management, CVD risk scoring, native kidney cancer surveillance
Nice to Know 🟢Novel immunosuppressants (vps34, JAK inhibitors), tolerance induction, xenotransplantation, machine perfusion, paired exchange algorithms, cost-effectiveness, long-term QoL

18. MCQs (10)

  1. Treatment of choice for ESRD:

A. Haemodialysis

B. Peritoneal dialysis

C. Kidney transplantation

D. Conservative management

E. CRRT

  1. Living donor minimum GFR:

A. 60 mL/min

B. 90 mL/min (measured)

C. 80 mL/min

D. 70 mL/min

E. 100 mL/min

  1. Induction agent for high immunologic risk (high PRA, retransplant, DSA):

A. Basiliximab

B. Anti-thymocyte globulin (ATG)

C. Alemtuzumab

D. No induction

E. Rituximab

  1. Standard maintenance immunosuppression (triple therapy):

A. Ciclosporin + Azathioprine + Prednisolone

B. Tacrolimus + Mycophenolate mofetil + Prednisolone

C. Sirolimus + MMF + Prednisolone

D. Tacrolimus + Azathioprine + Prednisolone

E. Belatacept + MMF + Prednisolone

  1. Tacrolimus target trough level (early post-transplant, 1–3 months):

A. 3–5 ng/mL

B. 8–12 ng/mL

C. 15–20 ng/mL

D. 5–8 ng/mL

D. 10–15 ng/mL

  1. Acute cellular rejection (Banff IA) — first-line treatment:

A. ATG

B. Methylprednisolone 500mg IV × 3 days

C. Plasma exchange

D. Rituximab

E. Increase MMF

  1. Antibody-mediated rejection — treatment:

A. Steroids alone

B. Plasma exchange + IVIG + rituximab

C. ATG alone

D. Increase tacrolimus

E. Splenectomy

  1. CMV prophylaxis highest risk group — duration:

A. D–/R–: 3 months

B. D+/R–: 3–6 months (valganciclovir)

C. D+/R+: 1 month

D. D–/R+: 6 months

E. No prophylaxis needed

  1. BK virus nephropathy — first step in management:

A. Start cidofovir

B. Reduce immunosuppression (reduce/stop MMF, reduce tacrolimus)

C. IVIG

D. Leflunomide

E. Switch to belatacept

  1. Post-transplant malignancy with highest relative risk:

A. Renal cell carcinoma

B. Skin cancer (SCC/BCC) — 50–100x

C. PTLD

D. Kaposi sarcoma

E. Cervical cancer

19. SBA Questions (10)

  1. 45-year-old man, ESRD on HD, potential living donor brother. Donor evaluation: GFR 95, ACR 1.5, BP 130/80, single renal artery. Suitable?

A. No (BP too high)

B. Yes (GFR>90, proteinuria<300mg, BP<140/90, single artery)

C. No (age >40)

D. No (related donor not preferred)

E. Only if ABO compatible

  1. Recipient with PRA 85%, previous transplant. Induction:

A. Basiliximab

B. ATG (high immunologic risk)

C. No induction

D. Alemtuzumab

E. Rituximab

  1. 3 months post-transplant, Cr rising from 110 to 180. Biopsy: Banff IB (t3, i3). No DSA, C4d negative. Treatment:

A. Plasma exchange

B. Methylprednisolone 500mg IV × 3–5 days

C. ATG (if steroid-resistant)

D. Rituximab

E. Increase tacrolimus only

  1. Same patient, biopsy shows v1 lesions (arterial inflammation). Treatment:

A. Steroids alone

B. ATG (vascular rejection)

C. Plasma exchange

D. Rituximab

E. Belatacept

  1. 6 months post-transplant, fever, pancytopenia, Cr rising. BK plasma PCR 50,000 copies/mL. Biopsy: SV40+. Management:

A. Cidofovir

B. Reduce MMF (stop), reduce tacrolimus target 4–6 ng/mL

C. Increase tacrolimus

D. ATG

E. Switch to belatacept

  1. D+/R– kidney transplant recipient. CMV prophylaxis:

A. No prophylaxis

B. Valganciclovir 900mg daily × 3–6 months

C. Aciclovir × 3 months

D. IV ganciclovir × 1 month

E. Pre-emptive monitoring only

  1. Transplant patient, 2 years post-Tx, on tacrolimus + MMF + pred 5mg. New skin lesion: biopsy SCC. Immunosuppression adjustment:

A. Stop all

B. Reduce/stop MMF; consider mTOR (sirolimus) conversion; dermatology referral

C. Increase tacrolimus

D. Stop prednisolone

E. Switch to belatacept

  1. Pregnancy planning in transplant recipient (2 years post-Tx, stable). Current: tacrolimus + MMF + pred. Action:

A. Stop all

B. Switch MMF → azathioprine pre-conception; continue tacrolimus + pred

C. Switch tacrolimus → sirolimus

D. Stop prednisolone

E. No change needed

  1. Early post-transplant (2 weeks), sudden anuria, Cr rising. Ultrasound: enlarged kidney, absent venous flow. Diagnosis:

A. Acute tubular necrosis

B. Renal vein thrombosis

C. Ureteric obstruction

D. Acute rejection

E. CNI toxicity

  1. Deceased donor: 55yo, HTN, died of CVA, Cr 1.6 mg/dL. KDPI category:

A. Standard criteria

B. Extended criteria donor (ECD) / KDPI >85%

C. DCD

D. Ideal donor

E. High KDPI but not ECD

20. Flashcards

  • Q: Treatment of choice ESRD?

A: Kidney transplantation

  • Q: Living donor GFR requirement?

A: >90 mL/min (measured)

  • Q: Living donor proteinuria limit?

A: <300 mg/day (ACR <3)

  • Q: Induction low risk?

A: Basiliximab

  • Q: Induction high risk?

A: ATG

  • Q: Maintenance standard?

A: Tac + MMF + Pred

  • Q: Tac trough early?

A: 8–12 ng/mL

  • Q: Tac trough late?

A: 5–8 ng/mL

  • Q: ACR Banff IA treatment?

A: Methylpred 500mg IV × 3d

  • Q: ACR vascular (v1+) treatment?

A: ATG

  • Q: AMR treatment?

A: PLEX + IVIG + rituximab

  • Q: CMV highest risk?

A: D+/R– (valganciclovir 3–6mo)

  • Q: PJP prophylaxis?

A: TMP-SMX 1 year

  • Q: BKVN screen?

A: PCR q3mo × 2yr

  • Q: BKVN threshold?

A: Plasma >10⁴ copies/mL

  • Q: BKVN 1st step?

A: Reduce IS (↓ MMF, ↓ Tac)

  • Q: Skin cancer risk?

A: 50–100x; dermatology q6–12mo

  • Q: PTLD?

A: EBV-driven; reduce IS 1st step

  • Q: NODAT risk?

A: Tacrolimus > ciclosporin; steroids

  • Q: Pregnancy wait?

A: ≥1 year post-Tx

  • Q: Pregnancy stop?

A: MMF, mTOR (sirolimus/everolimus)

  • Q: Pregnancy safe?

A: Tac, CsA, Pred, AZA

  • Q: Recurrent FSGS?

A: 30–50%; PLEX + rituximab

  • Q: ECD donor?

A: KDPI >85% (>60yo or 50-59 with HTN/CVA/Cr>1.5)

21. Answer Key with Explanations

MCQs

  1. C — Transplant = treatment of choice for ESRD
  2. B — Living donor GFR >90 (measured, not estimated)
  3. B — High immunologic risk = ATG
  4. B — Standard = Tac + MMF + Pred
  5. B — Early tac trough 8–12 ng/mL
  6. B — ACR IB = steroid pulse
  7. B — AMR = PLEX + IVIG + rituximab
  8. B — D+/R– highest CMV risk; valganciclovir 3–6mo
  9. B — BKVN = reduce immunosuppression first
  10. B — Skin cancer 50–100x risk

SBAs

  1. B — Brother: GFR 95, ACR 1.5, BP 130/80, single artery = ideal living donor
  2. B — PRA 85% + previous Tx = high risk = ATG
  3. B — Banff IB (t3, i3) = steroid pulse
  4. B — Vascular rejection (v1) = ATG
  5. B — BKVN = reduce IS (stop MMF, reduce Tac)
  6. B — D+/R– = valganciclovir 3–6mo
  7. B — SCC post-Tx = reduce/stop MMF, consider mTOR conversion, derm referral
  8. B — MMF teratogenic → switch to AZA pre-conception; Tac/Pred safe
  9. B — Early anuria + absent venous flow = renal vein thrombosis
  10. B — 55yo + HTN + CVA death + Cr 1.6 = ECD (KDPI >85%)

22. Summary

Kidney Transplantation is a Must Know 🔴 topic.

Key takeaway: Treatment of choice for ESRD. Live donor > deceased. Recipient workup: CV, infection (HIV/HBV/HCV/CMV/TB), malignancy, HLA/PRA/DSA, psychosocial. Living donor: GFR >90, proteinuria <300mg, BP <140/90, single artery. Induction: basiliximab (low risk) vs ATG (high risk/sensitised). Maintenance: Tac + MMF + Pred; Tac trough early 8–12, late 5–8. Rejection: ACR = steroid pulse ± ATG; AMR = PLEX + IVIG + rituximab. Infection: 0–1mo surgical, 1–6mo CMV/BK/PJP, >6mo community; prophylaxis: TMP-SMX, valganciclovir, aciclovir. Malignancy: skin 50–100x, PTLD (EBV), Kaposi (HHV-8). CVD: BP <130/80, statin (CNI interaction), NODAT. BK virus: PCR q3mo × 2yr; >10⁴ = BKVN → reduce IS. Recurrent FSGS: 30–50% → PLEX + rituximab. Pregnancy: wait ≥1yr; STOP MMF/mTOR; Tac/CsA/Pred/AZA safe.

Exam focus: Indications/workup, induction choice, tacrolimus targets, ACR vs AMR treatment, infection prophylaxis, malignancy screening, BK virus, recurrent FSGS, pregnancy management.

Clinical relevance: Transplant offers best survival/QoL; careful immunosuppression balance prevents rejection vs infection/malignancy; long-term follow-up essential.
